sections.label: Section 1.1251
sections.original: ἰὼ Γᾶ τε καὶ παμφαὴς
sections.translation: O earth , O sun whose beam illumines all , look , look upon this lost woman , ere she stretch forth , her murderous hand upon her sons for blood ;

sections.label: Section 1.1255
sections.original: τεᾶς γὰρ ἀπὸ χρυσέας γονᾶς
sections.translation: for lo ! these are scions of thy own golden seed , and the blood of gods is in danger of being shed by man . O light , from Zeus proceeding , stay her , hold her hand , forth from the house

sections.label: Section 1.1260
sections.original: τάλαινάν τ ’ Ἐρινὺν ὑπ ’ ἀλαστόρων .
sections.translation: chase this fell bloody fiend by demons led .

sections.label: Section 1.1261
sections.original: μάταν μόχθος ἔρρει τέκνων ,
sections.translation: Vainly wasted were the throes thy children cost thee ; vainly hast thou born , it seems , sweet babes , O thou who hast left behind thee that passage through the blue Symplegades , that strangers justly hate .

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.original: δειλαία , τί σοι φρενῶν βαρὺς
sections.translation: Ah ! hapless one , why doth fierce anger thy soul assail ? Why [*] in its place is fell murder growing up ? For grievous unto mortal men are pollutions that come of kindred blood poured on the earth ,

sections.label: Section 1.1270
sections.original: δὰ θεόθεν πίτνοντ ’ ἐπὶ δόμοις ἄχη .
sections.translation: woes to suit each crime hurled from heaven on the murderer ’ s house .

sections.label: Section 1270a
sections.original: αἰαῖ .

sections.label: Section 2.1273
sections.original: — ἀκούεις βοὰν ἀκούεις τέκνων ;
sections.translation: Didst hear , didst hear the children ’ s cry ? O lady , born to sorrow , victim of an evil fate !

sections.label: Section 2.1271
sections.original: — οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; ποῖ φύγω μητρὸς χέρας ;
sections.translation: ( within ). Ah , me ; what can I do ? Whither fly to escape my mother ’ s blows ?

sections.label: Section 2.1272
sections.original: — οὐκ οἶδ ’, ἄδελφε φίλτατ ’· ὀλλύμεσθα γάρ .
sections.translation: ( within ). I know not , sweet brother mine ; we are undone .

sections.label: Section 2.1275
sections.original: παρέλθω δόμους ; ἀρῆξαι φόνον
sections.translation: Shall I enter the house ? For the children ’ s sake I am resolved to ward off the murder .

sections.label: Section 2.1277
sections.original: — ναί , πρὸς θεῶν , ἀρήξατ ’· ἐν δέοντι γάρ .
sections.translation: ( within ). Yea , by heaven I adjure you ; help , your aid is needed .

sections.label: Section 2.1278
sections.original: — ὡς ἐγγὺς ἤδη γ ’ ἐσμὲν ἀρκύων ξίφους .
sections.translation: ( within ). Even now the toils of the sword are closing round us .

sections.label: Section 2.1279
sections.original: — τάλαιν ’, ὡς ἄρ ’ ἦσθα πέτρος ἢ σίδα-
sections.translation: O hapless mother , surely thou hast a heart of stone or steel

sections.label: Section 2.1280
sections.original: ρος , ἅτις τέκνων
sections.translation: to slay the offspring of thy womb by such a murderous doom .

sections.label: Section 2.1282
sections.original: — μίαν δὴ κλύω μίαν τῶν πάρος
sections.translation: Of all the wives of yore I know but one who laid her hand upon her children dear , even Ino , [*] whom the gods did madden in the day

sections.label: Section 2.1285
sections.original: δάμαρ νιν ἐξέπεμψε δωμάτων ἄλῃ ·
sections.translation: that the wife of Zeus drove her wandering from her home . But she , poor sufferer , flung herself into the sea because of the foul murder of her children , leaping o ’ er the wave-beat cliff , and in her death was she united to her children twain .

sections.label: Section 2.1290
sections.original: τί δῆτ ’ οὖν γένοιτ ’ ἂν ἔτι δεινόν ; ὦ
sections.translation: Can there be any deed of horror left to follow this ? Woe for the wooing of women fraught with disaster ! What sorrows hast thou caused for men ere now !

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: γυναῖκες , αἳ τῆσδ ’ ἐγγὺς ἕστατε στέγης ,
sections.translation: Ladies , stationed near this house , pray tell me is the author of these hideous deeds ,

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: Μήδεια τοῖσδ ’ ἔτ ’, ἢ μεθέστηκεν φυγῇ ;
sections.translation: Medea , still within , or hath she fled from hence ? For she must hide beneath the earth or soar on wings towards heaven ’ s vault , if she would avoid the vengeance of the royal house . Is she so sure she will escape herself unpunished from this house ,

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: ἀθῷος αὐτὴ τῶνδε φεύξεσθαι δόμων ;
sections.translation: when she hath slain the rulers of the land ? But enough of this ! I am forgetting her children . As for her , those whom she hath wronged will do the like by her ; but I am come to save the children ’ s life , lest the victim ’ s kin visit their wrath on me , in vengeance for the murder foul ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: μητρῷον ἐκπράσσοντες ἀνόσιον φόνον .
sections.translation: wrought by my children ’ s mother .

sections.label: Section 1306
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οὐκ οἶσθ ’ οἷ κακῶν ἐλήλυθας ,
sections.translation: Unhappy man , thou knowest not the full extent of thy misery , else had thou never said those words .

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: τί δ ’ ἔστιν ; ἦ που κἄμ ’ ἀποκτεῖναι θέλει ;
sections.translation: How now ? Can she want to kill me too ?

sections.label: Section 1309
sections.original: παῖδες τεθνᾶσι χειρὶ μητρῴᾳ σέθεν .
sections.translation: Thy sons are dead ; slain by their own mother ’ s hand .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: οἴμοι τί λέξεις ; ὥς μ ’ ἀπώλεσας , γύναι .
sections.translation: O God ! what sayest thou ? Woman , thou hast sealed my doom .

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: ὡς οὐκέτ ’ ὄντων σῶν τέκνων φρόντιζε δή .
sections.translation: Thy children are no more ; be sure of this .

sections.label: Section 1312
sections.original: ποῦ γάρ νιν ἔκτειν ’; ἐντὸς ἢ ἔξωθεν δόμων ;
sections.translation: Where slew she them ; within the palace or outside ?

sections.label: Section 1313
sections.original: πύλας ἀνοίξας σῶν τέκνων ὄψῃ φόνον .
sections.translation: Throw wide the doors and see thy children ’ s murdered corpses .

sections.label: Section 1314
sections.original: χαλᾶτε κλῇδας ὡς τάχιστα , πρόσπολοι ,
sections.translation: Haste , ye slaves , loose the bolts ,

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ἐκλύεθ ’ ἁρμούς , ὡς ἴδω διπλοῦν κακόν ,
sections.translation: undo the fastenings , that I may see the sight of twofold woe , my murdered sons and her , whose blood in vengeance I will shed . [ Medea in mid air , on a chariot drawn by dragons ; the children ’ s corpses by her .

sections.label: Section 1317
sections.original: τί τάσδε κινεῖς κἀναμοχλεύεις πύλας ,
sections.translation: Why shake those doors and attempt to loose their bolts , in quest of the dead and me their murderess ? From such toil desist . If thou wouldst aught with me ,

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: λέγ ’, εἴ τι βούλῃ , χειρὶ δ ’ οὐ ψαύσεις ποτέ .
sections.translation: say on , if so thou wilt ; but never shalt thou lay hand on me , so swift the steeds the sun , my father ’ s sire , to me doth give to save me from the hand of my foes .

sections.label: Section 1323
sections.original: ὦ μῖσος , ὦ μέγιστον ἐχθίστη γύναι
sections.translation: Accursed woman ! by gods , by me and all mankind abhorred as never woman was ,

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: ἥτις τέκνοισι σοῖσιν ἐμβαλεῖν ξίφος
sections.translation: who hadst the heart to stab thy babes , thou their mother , leaving me undone and childless ; this hast thou done and still dost gaze upon the sun and earth after this deed most impious . Curses on thee ! I now perceive what then I missed

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ὅτ ’ ἐκ δόμων σε βαρβάρου τ ’ ἀπὸ χθονὸς
sections.translation: in the day I brought thee , fraught with doom , from thy home in a barbarian land to dwell in Hellas , traitress to thy sire and to the land that nurtured thee . On me the gods have hurled the curse that dogged thy steps , for thou didst slay thy brother at his hearth

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: τὸ καλλίπρῳρον εἰσέβης Ἀργοῦς σκάφος .
sections.translation: ere thou cam ’ st aboard our fair ship Argo . Such was the outset of thy life of crime ; then didst thou wed with me , and having born me sons to glut thy passion ’ s lust , thou now hast slain them . Not one amongst the wives of Hellas e ’ er had dared

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ἔτλη ποθ ’, ὧν γε πρόσθεν ἠξίουν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: this deed ; yet before them all I chose thee for my wife , wedding a foe to be my doom , no woman , but a lioness fiercer than Tyrrhene Scylla in nature . But with reproaches heaped a thousandfold

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: δάκοιμι · τοιόνδ ’ ἐμπέφυκέ σοι θράσος ·
sections.translation: I cannot wound thee , so brazen is thy nature . Perish , vile sorceress , murderess of thy babes ! Whilst I must mourn my luckless fate , for I shall ne ’ er enjoy my new-found bride , nor shall I have the children ,

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ἕξω προσειπεῖν ζῶντας , ἀλλ ’ ἀπώλεσα .
sections.translation: whom I bred and reared , alive to say the last farewell to me ; nay , I have lost them .

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: μακρὰν ἂν ἐξέτεινα τοῖσδ ’ ἐναντίον
sections.translation: To this thy speech I could have made a long retort , but Father Zeus knows well all I have done for thee , and the treatment thou hast given me . Yet thou wert not ordained to scorn my love

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: τερπνὸν διάξειν βίοτον ἐγγελῶν ἐμοί ·
sections.translation: and lead a life of joy in mockery of me , nor was thy royal bride nor Creon , who gave thee a second wife , to thrust me from this land and rue it not . Wherefore , if thou wilt , call me e ’ en a lioness , and Scylla , whose home is in the Tyrrhene land ;

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: τῆς σῆς γὰρ ὡς χρὴ καρδίας ἀνθηψάμην .
sections.translation: for I in turn have wrung thy heart , as well I might .

sections.label: Section 1361
sections.original: καὐτή γε λυπῇ καὶ κακῶν κοινωνὸς εἶ .
sections.translation: Thou , too , art grieved thyself , and sharest in my sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1362
sections.original: σάφ ’ ἴσθι · λύει δ ’ ἄλγος , ἢν σὺ μὴ ’ γγελᾷς .
sections.translation: Be well assured I am ; but it relieves my pain to know thou canst not mock at me .

sections.label: Section 1363
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , μητρὸς ὡς κακῆς ἐκύρσατε .
sections.translation: O my children , how vile a mother ye have found !

sections.label: Section 1364
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ὡς ὤλεσθε πατρῴᾳ νόσῳ .
sections.translation: My sons , your father ’ s feeble lust has been your ruin !

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: οὔτοι νυν ἡμὴ δεξιά σφ ’ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: ’ Twas not my hand , at any rate , that slew them .

sections.label: Section 1366
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ὕβρις , οἵ τε σοὶ νεοδμῆτες γάμοι .
sections.translation: No , but thy foul treatment of me , and thy new marriage .

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.original: λέχους σφε κἠξίωσας οὕνεκα κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: Didst think that marriage cause enough to murder them ?

sections.label: Section 1368
sections.original: σμικρὸν γυναικὶ πῆμα τοῦτ ’ εἶναι δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: Dost think a woman counts this a trifling injury ?

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original: ἥτις γε σώφρων · σοὶ δὲ πάντ ’ ἐστὶν κακά .
sections.translation: So she be self-restrained ; but in thy eyes all is evil .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: οἵδ ’ οὐκέτ ’ εἰσί · τοῦτο γάρ σε δήξεται .
sections.translation: Thy sons are dead and gone . That will stab thy heart .

sections.label: Section 1371
sections.original: οἵδ ’ εἰσίν , οἴμοι , σῷ κάρᾳ μιάστορες .
sections.translation: They live , methinks , [*] to bring a curse upon thy head .

sections.label: Section 1372
sections.original: ἴσασιν ὅστις ἦρξε πημονῆς θεοί .
sections.translation: The gods know , whoso of them began this troublous coil .

sections.label: Section 1373
sections.original: ἴσασι δῆτα σήν γ ’ ἀπόπτυστον φρένα .
sections.translation: Indeed , they know that hateful heart of thine .

sections.label: Section 1374
sections.original: στύγει · πικρὰν δὲ βάξιν ἐχθαίρω σέθεν .
sections.translation: Thou art as hateful . I am aweary of thy bitter tongue .

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐγὼ σήν · ῥᾴδιον δ ’ ἀπαλλαγαί .
sections.translation: And I likewise of thine . But parting is easy .

sections.label: Section 1376
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ; τί δράσω ; κάρτα γὰρ κἀγὼ θέλω .
sections.translation: Say how ; what am I to do ? for I am fain as thou to go .

sections.label: Section 1377
sections.original: θάψαι νεκρούς μοι τούσδε καὶ κλαῦσαι πάρες .
sections.translation: Give up to me those dead , to bury and lament
